programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Bel and John are building a radical underground house on 25 spectacular acres at Whittlesea, in Victoria's Plenty Valley - so named for its fertile farmland and magnificent views. But the area has also acquired a more terrifying reputation. In 2009, 120 people died nearby at Kinglake in what's now known as the Black Saturday bushfires, and as a result Bel and John want a home that's fire proof! With John away at work for much of the build, it's up to Bel to project manage the difficult and complex build. But a number of unexpected challenges make the process harder than anticipated, and with their underground sanctuary looking more like an airport terminal than a stylish home, the pressure reaches breaking point!

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Rhonda Martin is a colourful woman who has stamped her bright personality all over her waterfront Coogee home. Her long-suffering husband Peter has painstakingly painted the ceiling roses to match the wallpaper but was over the house and Rhonda's decorating ideas years ago. Peter wants to sell up and hit the road in their camper van, however, Rhonda is yet to cut the grandkids apron strings. Two-and-a-half years ago, she agreed to put the house on the market - for $4.5 million. She's slowly coming round to Peter's way of thinking and a more realistic asking price of $3 million. But even with the knockout views, there aren't many buyers who will pay top dollar to live in a kaleidoscope.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Lyndsey and Eldridge move into Charlie's house after Alan burns down their home.

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, Truck Night goes back to the '90s, featuring a line-up of rigs from the millennium's last decade. An emotional story motivates one driver, and a razor-thin margin separates the two finalists when they face off in the Green Hell.

programme.sub-title: Energy Problems, Millennial Problems, Community Policing Problems
programme.desc: Wyatt tries to make coal miners go solar, move millennials into battleships, and understand community policing.

programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.sub-title: Adventures Of A Normal Man Pt. 1

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: After Cog informs Spawn of his shroud's powers, Spawn transforms himself into human form in order to visit Wanda. Twitch, recovering in the hospital, finds his memory foggy. Terry and Richard Sullivan try to expose Wynn's evil CIA dealings.

programme.title: Big Easy Motors
programme.desc: From a 1957 Chevy Bel Air convertible, to a 1967 Shelby Mustang GT, Charles Handler turns piles of junk into custom cars. He searches all over Louisiana, including the backwoods and the bayou, for classic cars no one wants, invests his own money to fix them up and restore them, and then sells them to the highest bidder. Their cars can sell for upwards of $50,000, so there's a huge profit to be made. But then again, if they put in the work and the car doesn't sell for what they expect it to, they could come home with no profit at all.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.desc: As the participants march deeper into winter, it becomes increasingly difficult to procure food. One participant gets a lucky break, but soon discovers things aren't what they seem. Another attempts to do battle with his own mental demons while another's time in the Arctic comes to an explosive finish.

programme.title: Haunted Case Files
programme.desc: All hell breaks loose when paranormal investigators disturb tortured spirits trapped in an old passenger train with a tragic past. One of New England's top ghost hunters is mercilessly toyed with by the demonic spirit of a child while exploring a haunted mansion, and the violent ghost of a school principal ruthlessly attacks a father-daughter ghost hunting team.

programme.title: Hotel Paranormal
programme.desc: A malicious presence attacks a bed-and-breakfast owner in the middle of the night, a couple awakens to a spirit shaking their bed, and a woman gets a bizarre phone call at a seedy motel.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Big Easy Motors
programme.desc: From a 1957 Chevy Bel Air convertible, to a 1967 Shelby Mustang GT, Charles Handler turns piles of junk into custom cars. He searches all over Louisiana, including the backwoods and the bayou, for classic cars no one wants, invests his own money to fix them up and restore them, and then sells them to the highest bidder. Their cars can sell for upwards of $50,000, so there's a huge profit to be made. But then again, if they put in the work and the car doesn't sell for what they expect it to, they could come home with no profit at all.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Celeste, Lydia West, Alastair Campbell: The competition continues as the artists create portraits and present them to the judges.

programme.title: Elon Musk's Twitter Takeover
programme.desc: The story of Elon Musk's long and often troubled relationship with Twitter, charting his journey from one of the site's most provocative users to its sole proprietor.

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey: What's Occurred
programme.desc: An uplifting and touching documentary special which tells the story of one of Britain's best loved sitcoms as it comes to an end after 17 years.

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael gets up close with a piece of natural history, visits a garden used as a viewing platform for public hangings, and experiences a timepiece like no other.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Jeremy Irons was born 19 September 1948 and is an English actor and activist. His first major film role came in the romantic drama The French Lieutenant's Woman (1981), for which he received a BAFTA nomination for Best Actor.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Just as critical to the success of the show as the writers, the animators had drama and tension of their own. The funky, punk rock animation house Klasky Csupo, led by Gabor Csupo had secured the contract of a lifetime, but could they hold on to The Simpsons? Through internal strife and natural disasters, the animators gave The Simpsons their signature look.

programme.title: History's Greatest Heists
programme.desc: Since the creation of currency, money has made the world go round and people have done anything and everything in their power to get their hands on a lot of it, including formulating some of the most devious and high-stakes heist attempts of all time. Using dramatic recreations, dynamic storytelling and cutting-edge visual effects, alongside first-person witness accounts from the people who were there, the series delves into the intricate schemes and audacity of the criminal masterminds who risked their freedom for a shot at a lifetime of wealth and riches. Brosnan, who is embedded into each heist through state-of-the-art technology, brings each global news headline to life by putting viewers at the heart of the action and breaking down every aspect of the plan including the conniving team, the mark, the execution and finally the aftermath.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: We follow the winner's progress as they head to Holland to research their 10,000 pound commission for the Queen's House at Royal Museums Greenwich.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo takes to the sea with the heroes of the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, visits a stormy coastal railway, and has a close personal encounter with his boyhood hero.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Just as critical to the success of the show as the writers, the animators had drama and tension of their own. programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Jeremy Irons was born 19 September 1948 and is an English actor and activist. His first major film role came in the romantic drama The French Lieutenant's Woman (1981), for which he received a BAFTA nomination for Best Actor.

programme.title: Europe's Waterways
programme.sub-title: Holland And Belgium - From Rotterdam To The Belgian Ardennes
programme.desc: Venturing upstream on Europe's waterways, the series discovers five regions of the continent from an unknown perspective and visits people who live from, on and with water. Rivers, canals, lake regions: they have shaped Europe's cultures for centuries - they are a place to live, transport arteries and sources of energy. Five journeys on watercourses broad and narrow lead to Italy, Germany, France, Poland, Holland and Belgium. Along the way: new projects for the future of water in Europe.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: The creative minds behind some of George Michael's most memorable music videos reveal how they brought his hits to life on screen.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: The story of how a little-known singer-songwriter from Paddington named Declan McManus became the world-famous Elvis Costello through his powerful introspective lyrics and innovative music videos.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: On August 9, 1969, 26-year-old Sharon and her unborn child are stabbed to death 16 times by a Satanist sect led by its self-appointed guru, Charles Manson.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: Tenerife - In The Realm Of Fire
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A luxurious 500 mile African train safari where we'll be travelling on Rovos Rail's vintage hotel train. Lovingly re-built by the Rovos family to the highest of specifications, this beautiful 14 carriage train leaves Pretoria for its three-day journey to Durban on the coast.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: Cricket thrives on partnerships, showcasing how two players can achieve greater success together than alone.

programme.title: Paul Kelly: Stories of me
programme.desc: This film charts the remarkable life of one of Australia's most gifted and beloved singer-songwriters. For almost 40 years, in over 350 songs, Paul Kelly has been mapping out the Australian landscape and its people. For the first time in a feature length film, the man behind the music is revealed, as Kelly speaks candidly about the people who have helped shape his life and music. Featuring rare archival footage, never-before-seen live performances, interviews with Kelly's family members, former and current band members, music contemporaries and many more.

programme.title: Bernadette Peters: Live In Australia
programme.desc: Broadway star Bernadette Peters headlines the 2009 Adelaide Cabaret Festival. From her opening number, Let Me Entertain You, Peters brings wit, emotion and her inimitable style to a range of musical numbers and pop classics. Highlights include a rendition of Fever from atop a grand piano, a heart-rending Rose's Turn (from Gypsy) and a performance of her self-penned charity single Kramer's Song.

Featuring rare archival footage, never-before-seen live performances, interviews with Kelly's family members, former and current band members, music contemporaries and many more.

programme.title: Bernadette Peters: Live In Australia
programme.desc: Broadway star Bernadette Peters headlines the 2009 Adelaide Cabaret Festival. From her opening number, Let Me Entertain You, Peters brings wit, emotion and her inimitable style to a range of musical numbers and pop classics. Highlights include a rendition of Fever from atop a grand piano, a heart-rending Rose's Turn (from Gypsy) and a performance of her self-penned charity single Kramer's Song.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: On August 9, 1969, 26-year-old Sharon and her unborn child are stabbed to death 16 times by a Satanist sect led by its self-appointed guru, Charles Manson.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: Tenerife - In The Realm Of Fire
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A luxurious 500 mile African train safari where we'll be travelling on Rovos Rail's vintage hotel train. Lovingly re-built by the Rovos family to the highest of specifications, this beautiful 14 carriage train leaves Pretoria for its three-day journey to Durban on the coast.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton sets out to experience island life today. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present.

programme.title: Branson

programme.title: Life Of Crime 1984-2020
programme.desc: The culmination of 36 years of work from multiple Emmy Award-winning producer/director Jon Alpert. Follow Rob, Freddie, and Deliris on a relentless ride through the streets and prisons of Newark, whose lives have been defined by and torn apart by their addictions. With unfettered access, the documentary bears witness to each of their journeys in and out of prison, rehab and in occasional jobs, as they struggle to end the vicious cycles of drug use and to connect with the families they left behind.

Bill Oakley and Josh Weinstein build on the legacy, expanding the world and exploring the side characters. To stay relevant, the Simpsons had to evolve; opening its doors to new creative voices, celebrity guest stars and even take on the big screen.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Dame Emma Thompson is a British actress, screenwriter, producer, activist, author, and comedian. One of Britain's most acclaimed actresses, she is the recipient of numerous accolades, including two Academy Awards and two Golden Globe Awards.

programme.title: History's Greatest Heists
programme.desc: Since the creation of currency, money has made the world go round and people have done anything and everything in their power to get their hands on a lot of it, including formulating some of the most devious and high-stakes heist attempts of all time. Using dramatic recreations, dynamic storytelling and cutting-edge visual effects, alongside first-person witness accounts from the people who were there, the series delves into the intricate schemes and audacity of the criminal masterminds who risked their freedom for a shot at a lifetime of wealth and riches. Brosnan, who is embedded into each heist through state-of-the-art technology, brings each global news headline to life by putting viewers at the heart of the action and breaking down every aspect of the plan including the conniving team, the mark, the execution and finally the aftermath.

Pascoe in particular is taking it all quite hard because he had promised the parents that justice would be done. When one of the bereaved parents kills Wheeler, Pascoe decides to cover up the crime.

programme.title: Ripper Street
programme.desc: Reid has finally captured Nathaniel and rid Whitechapel of its most feared monster since Jack the Ripper, but he still has one final enemy to defeat: Assistant Commissioner Augustus Dove. With Jackson's help, Reid must find sufficient forensic evidence to prove to Drummond - now in charge of Leman Street - that Dove killed the boy Robin Sumner. Even if Reid is able to bring Dove to justice, can he ever live in peace in Whitechapel - the place which haunts his dreams and nightmares?

programme.title: Dalziel and Pascoe
programme.desc: Peter is called out to investigate the murder of elderly Robert Deeks, an old soldier whose medals were also stolen. On the same night, Andy is accused of drunk driving, resulting in the death of an old cyclist, though Andy's friend, sober Arnie Bancroft, claims to have been the driver. With Andy on suspension, Peter looks into the murder and it turns out to be no coincidence that Mr Deeks' grandson Charley is in the same regiment as one Major Kassell, whom Andy suspects of being involved in a heroin-smuggling ring, and that Charley's fiancee Karen is a tarty waitress.

programme.title: Crumb
programme.desc: CRUMB examines the wide body of work of counter-culture hero and underground artist, Robert Crumb, discovering some of his sources of inspirations, including his upbringing.

programme.title: Eames: The Architect & The Painter
programme.desc: The life and work of the industrial designer Charles and Ray Eames.

programme.title: Trumbo
programme.desc: Forced into exile from Hollywood by Senator Joseph McCarthy's House Un-American Activities Committee, Trumbo continued to write by using several different pseudonyms, under which he produced some of his widely-acclaimed and award-winning screenplays. Letter-writing became the repository of Trumbo's extraordinary talents, and these correspondences serve as a wonderfully entertaining testament to the need for self-expression, social and political involvement, and remaining fallibly human in the face of barbarism. Told through performances of some of these extraordinary letters, by an award-winning cast of actors, archival and contemporary interviews and clips from his films, Trumbo illustrates how his unerring belief in the freedom of speech and the power of the written word allowed him to fight against political persecution.

programme.title: Railway Murders
programme.desc: The murder on 18th March 1910 of John Innes Nisbet, a colliery clerk from Heaton who had been carrying miners' pay for Stobswood Colliery. John Alexander Dickman was hanged for the crime.

programme.title: Kusama: Infinity
programme.desc: KUSAMA: INFINITY explores Yayoi Kusama's radical artistic vision and fierce determination to become a world-renowned artist. At 89 she is celebrated for her colourful polka-dot motifs and her immensely popular Infinity Mirrored Rooms. However, her critical and commercial success comes after decades of struggle against a traumatic upbringing, lifelong mental illness and sexism in the West.

programme.title: A Corner of the Earth

programme.title: Liam Gallagher: As It Was
programme.desc: Liam Gallagher: As It Was tells the honest and emotional story of how one of the most electrifying rock n' roll frontmen went from the dizzying heights of his champagne supernova years in Oasis to living on the edge, ostracised and lost in the musical wilderness of booze, notoriety and bitter legal battles. Starting again alone, stripped bare and with nowhere to hide, Liam risks everything to make the greatest rocknroll comeback of all time. Directors Charlie Lightening and Gavin Fitzgerald invite us backstage for a no-holdsbarred look at the life of one of the most talked-about and charismatic artists of his generation.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers get busy with batter, beating up wild waffles, an apple clafoutis and a 60-layer crepe cake.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul is now heading out to travel more widely across Japan by road, rail and air. On the road, he happens across a world record breaking bakery. On the railway, he lives out his ultimate train spotter fantasy rides the Shinkansen or bullet train as we know it and experiences the best railway buffet food on earth. And a short flight takes him to the sub tropical islands of Okinawa, once celebrated as the healthiest place in the world, but now becoming addicted to American style fast food. Paul also visits two of Japan's best known cities. First he visits Japan's ancient capital Kyoto where, as well as trying his hand at being a Ninja, meat lover Paul attempts to enjoy tofu in its many forms. Then he moves on to Hiroshima. Whilst most of the world only knows this city as the place they dropped the first atomic bomb, these days the city has a growing reputation across Japan for its food. Paul visits possibly the smallest restaurant in Hiroshima to meet its owner, a bomb survivor who continues to cook and serve the city's signature dish. In what Paul fondly remembers as one of his happiest and favourite days in Japan, she teaches Paul to cook the famous Hiroshima-style Okonimayaki. Finally, Paul travels way up north into the snow to take part in a particularly brutal rice festival. It's a contest where the north and south of the town fight it out with 6 metre long bamboo sticks to see whether they'll get a good rice harvest. Paul gets stuck into to both the fighting and the sake!

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: Masterchef UK: Celebrity

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: We get exclusive access to some of the largest food factories in Britain to reveal the secrets behind food production on an epic scale. We look at Britain's love of chocolate.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: We get exclusive access to some of the largest food factories in Britain to reveal the secrets behind food production on an epic scale. We look at Britain's love of chocolate.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Lancashire baker Molly, hopes to make a splash with her next cake as she's asked to bake a cake replica of a very special carp- the prize catch of angler Andrew, as a surprise for his 50th birthday. Wedding cake specialist Suzanne, takes a break from the norm as she's asked to make a 30th birthday cake for a modern art fan, who wants an edible installation as a centrepiece for his party. Built from 3 spherical tiers stacked on top of each other at precarious angles, Suzanne battles to get her ganache and fondant coverings smooth enough for the high gloss finish she needs to make this cake a success.

programme.desc: Join Justine in the French countryside, savouring mini croissants, exploring the world-famous Chablis wine at Domaine Laroche, and indulging in iconic madeleines.

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Justine relishes award-winning goat cheese at La Ferme d'Ulteria, crafts a fresh salad, and collaborates with Chef Leo for the Captain's Dinner in the historic Dordogne region.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's My Swedish Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel picks kale from the west coast of Sweden, and continues to the only large-scale tomato farm in Sweden. In her cabin, she makes a wonderful dish with butternut squash waffles and a mixed cherry tomato salad with crispy bacon.

programme.title: Legends Of The Fork
programme.desc: Buddy visits two generational legacies: family owned and operated Patsy's Italian Restaurant in New York City, and the famed Oaxacan joint in Los Angeles, Guelaguetza.

programme.desc: Andy is joined by Mark Lebrooy as they collaborate to create a flavoursome dish of crispy skin pork belly, expertly slow-cooked in Master stock and served with a delectable three-flavour sauce.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Michael is joined by Andy in the kitchen as they craft a delicious miso eggplant dish that tantalises the tastebuds. Next, Andy prepares a barbecue whole snapper.

programme.title: Paddock To Plate
programme.desc: Matt Moran explores the Riverland Region, hoping to snag a Murray Cod and release it back into the wild. programme.desc: First to host are Scottish tea room owner Melanie and her Irish husband David, who've blended together both their heritages with a Celtic fusion menu.

programme.desc: Jamie makes three one-pan delights: speedy smoked salmon pasta, a loaded beef burger with peppers and pickles, and soft sticky toffee apple buns.

programme.desc: Jamie cooks his favourite fish dishes, perfect for any summer supper.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: Rick visits Berlin, where history and the avant-garde dwell enticingly side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall teems now with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: Focusing on homegrown and seasonal produce, James takes a crop of fresh British flavours and transports them into recipes to inspire gardeners and home cooks alike. He is joined by Kate Garraway.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined in Northern Ireland by his good pal and local boy Paul Rankin. They brave the Carrick-a-Rede rope bridge, then James cooks a tasty fish dish with the dramatic coastline as his backdrop.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin's American Adventure continues with a cruise along California's Pacific Coast. James rides his motorcycle through the coastal city of Santa Cruz, taking in the fertile nature of the local farmland along the way. James shops at a local farmers' market before cooking his own recipes on a family run artichoke farm. But James couldn't leave before trying his hand at surfing, only to be upstaged by a surfing dog! James also uses the area's rich farming heritage as inspiration to cook California style back at home in the UK.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: This week, the hairy bikers are in god's county, Yorkshire and more especially the Yorkshire coast. The plan is simple, riding through this beautiful location and visiting top food producers who not only have amazing products but equally amazing stories to tell.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.title: How Do They Really Do It
programme.desc: This episode goes inside Primark to reveal how a small Irish department store became Britain's biggest clothing retailer, doubling their number of stores in the last 15 years, while the rest of the high street struggles. We go inside to find out the secrets of their success, from the buying team in Dublin HQ, to the world's biggest Primark in Birmingham. We discover how their unique approach to advertising gets them plenty of publicity without spending on TV ads. We see how their stores are carefully designed and curated to get customers in through the door and spending freely. We hear how Primark keeps their prices lower than the competition, and find out how their make up stands up against the big brands. And we hear exactly how Primark keeps their prices so low.

programme.title: River Cottage Summer Collection
programme.desc: Intrepid chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall brings a back-to-nature attitude to modern cooking and consumption. As his mission encompasses real people with real food issues, the host takes viewers into homes around Britain to show ordinary folks how easy and enjoyable it can be to move from pre-processed foods to nutritious natural products.

programme.title: River Cottage: Summer's Here
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall returns to River Cottage HQ to explore and celebrate the very best food that summer has to offer, including a lesson in sheep sheering and veal recipes.

programme.desc: With Christmas fast approaching Juliet turns her attention to baking with children. She makes a festive reindeer cake, a gingerbread house cake, a candy cane cake and snowmen cakes.

programme.desc: Jamie cooks his favourite fish dishes, perfect for any summer supper.

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: Raymond Blanc celebrates cheese in all its forms with Baked Camembert and Homemade Goat Cheese with Chicory and Walnut Salad. His old friend Claude Bosi joins him for a delicious meal.

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On
programme.desc: In unprecedented times, Jamie shares delicious recipes that use whatever ingredients you have at hand.

programme.title: River Cottage to the Core
programme.desc: Hugh decides to tackle some country and kitchen skills he has never undertaken before. It's all hands to the pump as he milks 48 ewes and sets out to catch his first sea trout, before creating a picnic for the local pub's sports day.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace follows 27 tonnes of potatoes from a farm in Hampshire through the largest crisp factory on Earth, as they are peeled, sliced and fried to make more than 5 million packets of crisps every 24 hours.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Specials
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty and Briony May Williams explore Nordic food, from cod farming in Norway to Swedish meatballs and Finnish cuisine, revealing how these dishes shape global tastes and happiness.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul is now heading out to travel more widely across Japan by road, rail and air. On the road, he happens across a world record breaking bakery. On the railway, he lives out his ultimate train spotter fantasy rides the Shinkansen or bullet train as we know it and experiences the best railway buffet food on earth. And a short flight takes him to the sub tropical islands of Okinawa, once celebrated as the healthiest place in the world, but now becoming addicted to American style fast food. Paul also visits two of Japan's best known cities. First he visits Japan's ancient capital Kyoto where, as well as trying his hand at being a Ninja, meat lover Paul attempts to enjoy tofu in its many forms. Then he moves on to Hiroshima. Whilst most of the world only knows this city as the place they dropped the first atomic bomb, these days the city has a growing reputation across Japan for its food. Paul visits possibly the smallest restaurant in Hiroshima to meet its owner, a bomb survivor who continues to cook and serve the city's signature dish. In what Paul fondly remembers as one of his happiest and favourite days in Japan, she teaches Paul to cook the famous Hiroshima-style Okonimayaki. Finally, Paul travels way up north into the snow to take part in a particularly brutal rice festival. It's a contest where the north and south of the town fight it out with 6 metre long bamboo sticks to see whether they'll get a good rice harvest. Paul gets stuck into to both the fighting and the sake!

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This lively group of Millennials think of each other as a family, they all agree that the best thing to come out COVID lockdowns was the time they spent together in the kitchen but with each flatmate returning to work, their love of fast food and quick-fix meals has snuck back in. Chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj are going to set some challenges for this busy household all in the hope of helping them to 'adult' better!

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three Welsh chefs compete over mains and desserts inspired by British animation and illustration and Paddington's 65th birthday, to get through to the regional final.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Love To Cook
programme.desc: Mary steps out with daughter Annabel, who shares her joy of foraging and cooking in the open, while rustling up her best recipes that offer the perfect comfort for family and friends.

programme.title: Gino's Italy: Secrets of the South
programme.desc: Gino travels to the fortress port of Gallipoli, famous for its raw fish consumption and the melting pot of Puglia.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Fantastic Feast
programme.desc: Dame Mary Berry knows a thing or two about cooking up a Fantastic Feast, so she's sharing her cooking skills with 3 novice cooks - Rima, her mother-in-law Diane, and her best friend Noreen, who want to throw a spectacular surprise 30th birthday feast for Rima's husband Mark. With the help of Dame Mary, can they pull it off and give Mark a birthday surprise he'll never forget?

programme.title: MasterChef UK
programme.desc: Tonight, the contestants face two more challenges, designed to gauge both their creativity and culinary repertoire. Where the seven are tasked with inventing a dish on the spot for judges from a market full of ingredients, which include poussin, clams and whole bream.

programme.title: Bryan's All In
programme.desc: Rob and Lorraine have become full-time dragon slayers, fixing a slew of reno surprises. Bryan helps renovate their suite with the highest income-generating potential.

programme.title: Grand Designs UK
programme.desc: Rebecca has returned home to Cornwall and has a highly ambitious plan. Together with her partner, they will transform a small, damp, bungalow into a cutting-edge family home for their three-year-old daughters - all for just 80,000 pounds.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Confronted by delays on the pool project, Julia completes her wedding and events space. Tim and Krys discover one of the Dordogne's local delicacies. Anna and Philipp struggle to complete their fourth suite.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: With their children flying the nest, Andy and Alida have decided now's the time to fulfil their dream of buying a chalet in the French Alps.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Tony and Hendrik hunt for their dream holiday home on Portugal's Setubal Peninsula for $330,000. Can Danni Menzies find the perfect place for all their family to enjoy?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retirees Annette and Malcom are ready to embark on a new adventure in Almeria in Spain, where they plan to soak up the winter sun. Leah Charles-King shows them what their 200,000 pounds can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi heads to Poitou-Charentes in the west of France to help house hunters Jules and Gaynor. Along with their pet alpacas, they are planning a permanent move to rural France.

programme.title: Love It or List It Vancouver

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Boat lover Laurence Kent has always lived by the water. Previously living on a small narrowboat moored in his own marina in East Farleigh, he had dreams of an upgraded pad. A comfortable living space on the water, that was big enough for his grandson to have a sleepover but small enough to fit in his marina. Laurence put his faith in the hands of Richard Homewood, an engineer that has designed a new floating home with a bespoke micro- home, called The Riverpod. To save costs, Laurence hopes to fit out the interior himself and with a background in renovation, he would like to re-design the layout of the Pod, offering a larger bedroom and bigger open plan kitchen/living area. Over in Portland, Oregon, Arizonian action duo, Jan and Don Hansen, love to holiday on the Willamette River in Oregon, so they'll design and build the ultimate floating summerhouse, giving them unparalleled access to the water. Costing US $450,000, they want vast outdoor decking, a custom built steel and wood staircase, a wood burning stove and an open plan living space.

programme.sub-title: Cheryl Hines Gifts A Garage Makeover
programme.desc: Emmy nominated actress Cheryl Hines is showing her gratitude to her close friend of nearly 20 years with a renovation. She teams up with Jonathan and Drew to transform an underused garage into a guest suite.

programme.title: Fixer to Fabulous
programme.desc: A family that survived a devastating house fire buys a home on the lake so they can enjoy their favourite spot year-round. However, the lack of space doesn't fit their needs, so they ask Dave and Jenny to completely overhaul the rustic cabin.

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home
programme.desc: Two Bostonians couldn't have wished for a better start on the property ladder than a share in a million-dollar lotto win. With David as their guide, they tour gorgeous century-old houses full of history.

programme.title: My Lottery Dream Home
programme.desc: Richmond is for lovers and winners now that a couple are using their $100,000 win to put a down payment on their first family home in Virginia. David narrows down the options.

programme.title: Flip or Flop
programme.desc: Tarek and Christina get a lead on a home in Garden Grove, California, that is in need of a major makeover. But will this add or subtract from their profits?

programme.title: Flip or Flop
programme.desc: Tarek and Christina find a house in Buena Park, California. Its large lot could mean extra profits but only if they can split and sell it separately from the house.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Forever Home
programme.desc: Lisa, John and their two grown kids Cora and Spencer have lived in their home for over 20 years. But with the choppy layout, cramped kitchen and dated style, the house is no longer functioning for four adults living under the same roof, and the family needs help.

programme.title: Storm Boy
programme.desc: A young boy rescues and raises three orphaned pelicans. But when he forms a close bond with them, he finds himself at odds with his fisherman father.

programme.title: Brokeback Mountain
programme.desc: Set against the sweeping landscapes of Wyoming and Texas, this epic love story tells of two young men - a ranch-hand and a rodeo cowboy - who meet in the summer of 1963 while driving cattle on a mountain range. They unexpectedly forge a lifelong connection, one whose complications, joys and tragedies provide a testament to the endurance and power of love.

programme.title: Paul
programme.desc: Two geeks go on a pilgrimage which takes them to America's UFO heartland. While there, they accidentally meet an alien who brings them on an insane road trip that alters their universe forever. For the past 60 years, an alien named Paul has been hanging out at a top-secret military base. When he discovers he's been taken prisoner, the space-traveling smart ass decides to escape the compound and hop on the first vehicle out of town-a rented RV containing Earthlings Graeme Willy and Clive Gollings. Chased by federal agents and the fanatical father of a young woman that they accidentally kidnap, Graeme and Clive hatch a fumbling escape plan to return Paul to his mother ship. And as two nerds struggle to help, one little green man might just take his fellow outcasts from misfits to intergalactic heroes.

programme.title: Inglourious Basterds
programme.desc: As war rages in Europe, a Nazi-scalping squad of American soldiers, known to their enemy as The Basterds, is on a daring mission to take down the leaders of the Third Reich.

programme.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 - Parabellum
programme.desc: Retired super-assassin John Wick returns with a $14 million price tag on his head and an army of bounty-hunting killers on his trail. The killing of a member of the shadowy international assassins guild known as the High Table leaves John Wick excommunicado. In a lethal world of blood oaths and ever-changing alliances, John is dogged by the most ruthless hitmen and women in the world. Could this be John Wick's last stand?

programme.title: Gladiator
programme.desc: Forced into slavery by the corrupt heir to the throne, Roman general Maximus becomes a gladiator. His prowess in the arena leads him to Rome, the Colosseum, and a showdown with the new emperor.

programme.title: From Paris with Love
programme.desc: James Reece, personal aide to the US ambassador to France, is secretly moonlighting as a low-level CIA operative. Looking for more action, Reece accepts a job that teams him with wisecracking special agent Charlie Wax, a trigger-happy loose cannon sent to Paris on a mission of international importance. Now, Reece finds himself on the wildest ride of his life as the new partners pull out all the stops to annihilate the enemy.

programme.title: Manchester by the Sea
programme.desc: This film tells the story of the Chandlers, a working-class family from Massachusetts. After Lee's older brother, Joe, suddenly passes away, he is made the legal guardian of his nephew. Lee is forced to deal with a tragic past that separated him from his wife, Randi, and the community where he was born and raised.

programme.title: Born on the Fourth of July
programme.desc: An idealistic young American proudly enlists during the Vietnam War and returns home a quadriplegic. Struggling to overcome drug addiction and depression, he turns his life around and becomes an outspoken critic of the war. Based on the true story of Ron Kovic.

programme.title: The Secret Garden
programme.desc: When Mary Lennox's parents suddenly die, she is sent to live with her uncle, Archibald Craven, on his remote country estate deep in the Yorkshire moors. While exploring, she discovers a hidden magical garden.

programme.title: Monster House
programme.desc: Horace Nebbercracker is the meanest man in town, heaven forbid anything that lands on his lawn or he'll snatch it away forever. DJ's obsession with spying on the creepy house across the street has made him a bit of an outcast, but he's determined to get to the bottom of the mystery.

programme.title: Home
programme.desc: When Oh, a loveable misfit from another planet, lands on Earth and finds himself on the run from his own people, he forms an unlikely friendship with an adventurous girl named Tip who is on a quest of her own. Through a series of comic adventures with Tip, Oh comes to understand that being different and making mistakes is all part of being human. And while he changes her planet and she changes his world, they discover the true meaning of the word home.

programme.title: Jurassic World
programme.desc: The Jurassic World theme park lets guests experience the thrill of witnessing actual dinosaurs, but something ferocious lurks behind the park's attractions - a genetically modified dinosaur with savage capabilities. When the massive creature escapes, chaos erupts across the island. Now it's up to Owen and Claire to save the park's tourists from an all-out prehistoric assault.

programme.title: Jaws 2
programme.desc: Just when you thought it was safe to go back in the water, another shark terrorises the resort of Amity as it recovers from the first man-eater. Police Chief Brody returns to kill.

programme.title: Jason Bourne
programme.desc: Former CIA assassin Jason Bourne resurfaces just as his former employers are implementing a new program aimed at hunting him down. Now fully recovered from his amnesia, Bourne teams up with Nicky Parsons to uncover the truth behind his father's death, while simultaneously exacting revenge and evading the government forces hot on his trail.

programme.title: Robin Hood
programme.desc: Robin Hood, the legendary figure of 13th century England, who along with his band of marauders confront corruption in the local village of Nottingham and lead an uprising against the crown that will forever alter the balance of world power. Whether regarded as thief or hero, one man from humble beginnings will become an eternal symbol of freedom for his people.

programme.title: The Mummy
programme.desc: Adventurer Rick O'Connell and Egyptologist Evelyn Carnahan discover the lost city of Hamunaptra, and struggle to save the world from unspeakable evil after they inadvertently stumble upon a mummy's tomb in 1923, and the mummy is accidentally resurrected.

programme.title: 2 Fast 2 Furious
programme.desc: It's the nitro-fuelled answer to the question: how fast do you like it? Now an ex-cop on the run, Brian O'Connor hooks into outlaw street-racing. When the Feds strong-arm him back, O'Connor's no rules, win-or-die skills are unleashed against an international drug lord. With his velocity-addicted buddy riding shotgun, and a drop-dead-gorgeous undercover agent dialling up the heat, this film accelerates the action into a desperate race for survival, justice and mind-blowing, jaw-dropping speed.

programme.title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park
programme.desc: It has been four years since the disaster at Jurassic Park and two groups are in a race against time that will determine the fate of the remote island's prehistoric inhabitants.

programme.title: The Eagle
programme.desc: In 140 AD, 20 years after the unexplained disappearance of the entire Ninth Legion in the mountains of Scotland, young centurion Marcus Aquila arrives from Rome to solve the mystery and restore the reputation of his father, the commander of the Ninth. Accompanied only by his British slave Esca, Marcus sets out across Hadrian's Wall into the uncharted highlands of Caledonia to confront its savage tribes, make peace with his father's memory, and retrieve the lost legion's golden emblem, the Eagle of the Ninth.

programme.title: Erin Brockovich
programme.desc: Erin Brockovich, a struggling single mum who exposes a powerful utility company for dumping deadly toxic waste which is slowly poisoning the local residents. Employed as a filing clerk in her lawyer's firm, the former beauty queen stumbles across this landmark case that transforms her into a working class hero.

programme.title: Chicken Run
programme.desc: While the chickens on evil Mrs Tweedy's farm dream of a better life, a clever hen named Ginger is hatching plans to fly the coop for good. The only problem is, chickens can't fly or can they? Every escape attempt goes fowl until Rocky, a smooth-talking All-American rooster, crash-lands into the coop. It's hardly poultry in motion when Rocky attempts to teach Ginger and her fine feathered friends to fly but, with teamwork, determination and a little bit of cluck, the fearless flock plots one last daring attempt in a spectacular bid for freedom! Featuring unforgettable characters, incredible animation and all-star voice talent, this instant classic from the academy Award-winning creators of Wallace & Gromit is eggs-traordinary fun for the whole family!

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Welcome to the Hotel Transylvania. Dracula, who runs a five-stake resort away from the human world, goes into overprotective mode when a boy discovers the resort and falls for the count's daughter.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2
programme.desc: Worried that his half-vampire grandson isn't showing signs of being a vampire, Dracula enlists his friends to put him through 'monster-in-training' boot camp.

programme.title: Evan Almighty
programme.desc: Newly elected to Congress, Evan Baxter has his life turned upside-down when God appears and mysteriously commands him to build an ark. However, his perplexed family can't decide whether Evan is having an extraordinary mid-life crisis or is truly onto something of Biblical proportions.

programme.title: Tootsie
programme.desc: Michael Dorsey is a talented actor, but his demanding nature and stubborn temperament have antagonised every producer in New York. Now his agent insists no one will hire him. But Michael needs money, eight thousand dollars to be exact, and to earn it, he's willing to play the role of a lifetime. This out-of-work actor finds success, love and frustration when he gets the role of his life playing a woman.

programme.title: Jerry Maguire
programme.desc: A sports agent suddenly discovers his scruples and promptly loses his job. But with the help of one loyal colleague and one outrageous client, he learns that loving well is the best revenge.

programme.title: The Da Vinci Code
programme.desc: A Harvard professor implicated in a murder must unravel an ancient code to expose the greatest conspiracy in history.

programme.title: The Breakfast Club
programme.desc: When Saturday detention started, they were the Jock, the Princess, the Brain, the Criminal, and the Basket Case, but by that afternoon they had become closer than any of them could have imagined.

programme.title: Friday Night Lights
programme.desc: A besieged high school football coach works with a losing team in a Texan town where winning is everything. The true story of the 1988 Permian Panthers, the most successful team in state history, whose players all but buckle under the pressure of their needy fans. The unique power of this film lies in asking as many questions of a sports-mad culture as it answers.

programme.title: Jaws
programme.desc: The thriller that scared movie fans everywhere out of the water. Three men undertake a perilous journey to hunt down a great white killer shark.

programme.title: The Dilemma
programme.desc: A man finds out that his best friend and business partner's wife may be cheating, plunging him into the moral dilemma of deciding whether to tell his friend. What ensues is comic mayhem wherein the process of uncovering lies only begets more lies.

programme.title: Grown Ups 2
programme.desc: Lenny has relocated his family back to the small town where he and his friends grew up. This time around, the grown ups are the ones learning lessons from their kids on a day notoriously full of surprises: the last day of school.

programme.title: Gandhi
programme.desc: His goal was freedom. His strategy was peace. His weapon was humanity. This film is an epic masterpiece that traces the historic steps of this revered man.

programme.title: Home
programme.desc: When Oh, a loveable misfit from another planet, lands on Earth and finds himself on the run from his own people, he forms an unlikely friendship with an adventurous girl named Tip who is on a quest of her own. Through a series of comic adventures with Tip, Oh comes to understand that being different and making mistakes is all part of being human. And while he changes her planet and she changes his world, they discover the true meaning of the word home.

programme.title: Curious George: Royal Monkey
programme.desc: Join your favourite monkey as he takes on his most regal role yet, trading places with a royal monkey.

programme.title: Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron
programme.desc: A tale which follows the adventures of a wild and rambunctious mustang stallion as he journeys through the untamed American frontiers, encountering man for the first time, Spirit defies being broken, even as he develops a remarkable friendship with a young Lakota brave named Little Creek. The young stallion also finds love with a beautiful paint mare named Rain on his way to becoming one of the greatest unsung heroes of the Old West.

programme.title: Massive Engineering Mistakes
programme.desc: In downtown San Francisco, what's causing a skyscraper to sink? In Venice, a biblical build fails to hold back the tide, a breath-taking bridge in Bilbao becomes a crossing catastrophe and in Germany, a town that's in danger of going underground.

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Myths and Legends
programme.desc: Jonathan Ross goes in search of Britain's lost legends. In Whitby, where Dracula landed, he learns about mythical cave-dwelling creatures with healing powers. He then heads to the Moors.

programme.title: Air Wars
programme.desc: In depth analysis of the major air battles of WWII with unique archive, animated graphics and portraits of the leaders of all sides.

programme.title: History of Warfare
programme.desc: After months of rising tension, The English Civil War finally exploded into life on the slopes of Edgehill on October 23, 1642. It was to be a confused and bitter business, marked by the foolishness of Prince Rupert and the Royalist cavalry, who left the field at a crucial stage of the battle. As darkness fell, nearly 3000 men lay dead and the die was cast: the English Civil War would be no short and decisive affair.

programme.title: The Fury and the Flames
programme.desc: Featuring remarkable archive footage, this is a powerful account of Germany's feared and elite Parachute Divisions.

programme.title: Dogfights
programme.desc: They fight in a supersonic world, where split-second decisions determine if they live - or die. American F4 Phantom pilots Fred Olmsted and Dan Cherry take on the famed MiG-21: the most feared threat in the sky.

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: A Northampton family, the Colecloughs live on the site of a 900-year-old nunnery with a rather fruity past, which was at various times accused of witchcraft, begging and debauchery. The burial of a much loved family pet provides the curious starting point for an investigation. When eight-year-old Amy Coleclough's cat Paintpot died and her father dug a grave, he uncovered a large old wall. So Tony and the team dig up their garden, including a mysterious sarcophagus hidden there.

programme.title: Coast Australia
programme.desc: Not only where the desert dramatically meets the sea, historically, with the colony on Australia's east coast set, and growing on the west coast the GAB became a crucial link between the two, with many Australian firsts, in travel, transport and communication. Now with a marine park of immense significance, and locations like the Bunda Cliffs giving insights into life, and earths movements dating back to the supercontinent Gondwanaland, the Great Australian Bight is diverse, dazzling and captivating. The GAB is crucial for the breeding of the Southern Wright Whale, with these whales steeped in Aboriginal mythology and now part of a major scientific study, Emma drops in on some whale mothers and their calves. Tim investigates experimentation in the atom bomb on Australian soil, and it's after effects, that uprooted an entire community; and Dean risks diving with the sharks for some ocean gold; abalone.

programme.title: World's Greatest Ships
programme.desc: Rob Bell explores the extraordinary story of the Golden Hind - the Flagship of England's most notorious pirate, Sir Francis Drake. Sent on a secret mission to plunder Spanish ships in the Pacific, the Golden Hind's voyage was packed with adventure: from witchcraft and attempted mutiny, to the supreme triumph of her most famous achievement - becoming the first English Ship to sail around the world. Rob climbs aboard a replica of the Golden Hind on London's South Bank to get to grips with the human stores behind this remarkable vessel. He samples weevil-ridden sailor's rations, gets a crash course in Elizabethan firearms and learns how paranoia and suspicion led Drake to execute one of his closest friends. And, when it came to taking on formidable Spanish galleons, Rob learns how Drake put the Golden Hind's small size to good use - using stealth and cunning to rob untold riches from unsuspecting enemy ships. Most remarkably, Rob learns how Drake's circumnavigation of the globe was not only an accident, but also a state secret that he might have taken to the grave - had it not been for the surprise intervention of Queen Elizabeth herself.

programme.title: World's Greatest Ships
programme.desc: Rob uncovers the incredible story behind a humble British merchant ship that changed the world - The Mayflower. He finds out how a group of religious dissidents- the Pilgrims hire the ship to undertake a desperate voyage to the New World. Facing fierce storms, mutiny, deprivation and death the Mayflower's crew and the Pilgrims overcame almost impossible odds to establish the colony that ultimately gave birth to the United States of America.

programme.title: Mauthausen: Camp of No Return
programme.desc: Behind the horror of concentration camps lies an underground industry. The prisoners in Mauthausen and the neighbouring camps were exploited to the death in order to serve the ambitions of the Nazi regime. This investigative documentary features miraculous archive footage and poignant testimonies, revealing the daily hell of one of the deadliest concentration camps of World War II.

programme.title: Baby Cages
programme.desc: An original and little-known re-education program starts in a POW camp in France. The aim is to steer captured Hitler youth members towards an attitude of tolerance and democracy. It was called Baby Cages.

programme.title: Coast Australia
programme.desc: Not only where the desert dramatically meets the sea, historically, with the colony on Australia's east coast set, and growing on the west coast the GAB became a crucial link between the two, with many Australian firsts, in travel, transport and communication. programme.desc: The team unearth the secrets of a Cotswolds field. Roman coins and bits of masonry have been found on this land, but it's the chance discovery of a piece of mosaic floor that has really got the archaeologists excited.

Facing fierce storms, mutiny, deprivation and death the Mayflower's crew and the Pilgrims overcame almost impossible odds to establish the colony that ultimately gave birth to the United States of America.

programme.title: Mauthausen: Camp of No Return
programme.desc: Behind the horror of concentration camps lies an underground industry. The prisoners in Mauthausen and the neighbouring camps were exploited to the death in order to serve the ambitions of the Nazi regime. This investigative documentary features miraculous archive footage and poignant testimonies, revealing the daily hell of one of the deadliest concentration camps of World War II.

programme.title: Baby Cages
programme.desc: An original and little-known re-education program starts in a POW camp in France. The aim is to steer captured Hitler youth members towards an attitude of tolerance and democracy. It was called Baby Cages.

programme.title: Steam Train Journeys
programme.desc: What could be more quintessentially British, than a traditional afternoon tea, on board a heritage steam train? At the South Devon Railway, veteran silver service waiter Andy Leech and head chef Liz Turner are preparing to serve an elegant afternoon extravaganza to 44 lucky passengers - all while balancing on a moving train. Meanwhile, at the other end of the line, locomotive manager Ray Lee joins old technology with new, as he attempts to get a 45-tonne loco on and off a brand-new low loader. At the East Lancashire Railway, volunteer Chandra Law, having worked tirelessly at Ramsbottom Station for the past twenty years, has just been promoted to top job of Stationmaster. But to succeed in her new role, she needs to master the art of their ticketing system, and the daunting task of balancing the books at the end of the day.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Lighthouses
programme.desc: Presenter Rob Bell takes us on a voyage around Britain and Ireland to reveal the hidden secrets that make offshore lighthouses such extraordinary feats of engineering.

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The team unearth the secrets of a Cotswolds field. Roman coins and bits of masonry have been found on this land, but it's the chance discovery of a piece of mosaic floor that has really got the archaeologists excited.

programme.title: The Irish in Australia
programme.desc: Writer, actress and comedian Fiona O'Loughlin, mother of five and third generation Australian, was conscious of her Irish heritage but not sure what it meant. Her eight great-grandparents migrated from Ireland in the closing decades of the 19th century, but what influence has that fact had on Fiona? More broadly, what influence has Irish migration had on Australia's way of life? As Fiona puts it on her journey from coast to coast: what have the Irish ever done for us? A question she asks of writers, comedians, historians, nuns and bushmen as she discovers the stories of settlers, survivors, saints, squatters, sports stars, storytellers, sermonisers, sinners and scoundrels.

programme.title: The Royals: A History of Scandals
programme.desc: Professor Suzannah Lipscomb explores the scandals that break when Royals spending gets out of control. From Henry VIII's Field of Cloth of Gold to the extraordinary debt that George IV found himself in.

programme.title: Inside Japan's War
programme.desc: On the day that Nagasaki is bombed, the Trans Baikal and 2nd Far East Fronts of the Red Army invade Manchuria, and the 1st Far East Front move from Vladivostok.

programme.title: The Dust Bowl
programme.desc: THE DUST BOWL chronicles the worst man-made ecological disaster in American history, in which the frenzied wheat boom of the "Great Plow-Up," was followed by a decade-long drought during the 1930s nearly swept away the breadbasket of the nation. Vivid interviews with twenty-six survivors of those hard times, combined with dramatic photographs and seldom seen movie footage, bring to life stories of incredible human suffering and equally incredible human perseverance. It is also a morality tale about our relationship to the land that sustains us-a lesson we ignore at our peril.

programme.title: Waterloo's Warriors
programme.desc: The Duke of Wellington would not have defeated Napoleon at the Battle of Waterloo without the valour and brilliance of his non-English troops. Now, for the first time, their extraordinary stories of heroism and tragedy are told onscreen.

programme.title: The Whistle-Blowers of WWII
programme.desc: With the help of never-before-seen archive material, this documentary demonstrates how diplomats tried to warn the world of the increasing dangers and reality of Nazi Germany to no avail.

programme.title: The Royals: A History of Scandals
programme.desc: Professor Suzannah Lipscomb explores the scandals that break when Royals spending gets out of control. From Henry VIII's Field of Cloth of Gold to the extraordinary debt that George IV found himself in.

programme.title: Inside Japan's War
programme.desc: On the day that Nagasaki is bombed, the Trans Baikal and 2nd Far East Fronts of the Red Army invade Manchuria, and the 1st Far East Front move from Vladivostok.

programme.title: The Whistle-Blowers of WWII
programme.desc: With the help of never-before-seen archive material, this documentary demonstrates how diplomats tried to warn the world of the increasing dangers and reality of Nazi Germany to no avail.

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The team delve into the recent past to uncover the hidden archaeology behind the biggest battle that never was, the planned defence of Britain against a Nazi invasion in 1940. Along the busy main roads and in the quiet back gardens of a London suburb they uncover evidence of anti-tank weapons, massive flame throwers and secret bunkers in the quiet gardens of a London suburb, speak to members of the Home Guard who recall their defensive training, and reveal how the British Resistance would have reacted if the Nazi invasion had been a success.

programme.title: The Irish in Australia

programme.title: The World from Above
programme.sub-title: Lime Rock Park To Faulkner's Lighthouse
programme.desc: A breathtaking series of aerial programs offering an entirely different view of the world. From 3km down to just a few metres above ground, discover new perspectives through thrilling journeys: from mountains and great cities, to fantastic castles and the great natural wonders of the globe.

programme.title: The World from Above
programme.sub-title: Maine - Isles Of Shoals To Bath
programme.desc: The World From Above reveals our globe's most beautiful and dramatic places.

programme.title: The World from Above

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: The team delve into the recent past to uncover the hidden archaeology behind the biggest battle that never was, the planned defence of Britain against a Nazi invasion in 1940. Along the busy main roads and in the quiet back gardens of a London suburb they uncover evidence of anti-tank weapons, massive flame throwers and secret bunkers in the quiet gardens of a London suburb, speak to members of the Home Guard who recall their defensive training, and reveal how the British Resistance would have reacted if the Nazi invasion had been a success.

programme.title: Diana: The Ultimate Truth
programme.desc: More than two decades on, the circumstances around Princess Diana's death continue to create international headlines. A global icon, arguably the most famous woman in the world, killed aged just 36, in a car crash in a Paris underpass. Despite a verdict of unlawful killing and two police investigations into the devastating crash, many questions around what happened, and who is responsible, remain.

programme.title: Crime 360
programme.desc: Crime 360 takes viewers inside the investigation as theories and evidence are brought to life through fantastic CGI visualizations, state-of-the-art 3D laser scanning and 360-degree digital photography. In each episode, we follow one case from start to finish. From the moment detectives are called to the scene, viewers are part of the investigation, experiencing the crime scene from every angle, and the evidence at the microscopic level. As the case develops, and the theories change, the graphics evolve, culminating in a full-blown visualization of what really happened.

programme.title: Hours To Kill
programme.desc: The key to solving the toughest homicides lies somewhere within the final 24 hours of the victim's life. This true crime series follows investigators as they piece together events.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: Police find more than treats in a suspect's pocket. An armed driver tells officers a far-fetched story to justify his weapon. A heated back and forth ends with a call to animal control.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A stolen vehicle is overturned during a pursuit. A young intoxicated man grapples with officers. Officers work to disarm a gunman. A talkative drunk female driver is arrested on multiple charges.

programme.title: Night Coppers
programme.sub-title: I've Got Pubes Older Than You
programme.desc: Rookie cops Matt and Will stop an ex-police boss by mistake, then tackle a spiking case. Sophie and Rachel investigate a street assault, while Steve finds a pair of legs in the bushes.

programme.title: #textmewhenyougethome
programme.desc: #TextMeWhenYouGetHome became a viral, worldwide movement following the 2021 death of Sarah Everard. The slogan sparked awareness around the vulnerability women feel while out in public alone. In this gripping, true-crime docuseries, each episode follows the case of an innocent woman who has been abducted, harmed or even killed while she was out by herself. Emotional interviews by loved-ones drive these stories; accompanied by stylistic recreations, actual texts, phone records and other digital breadcrumbs that authorities used to solve the case. Keeping the mystery alive is the whodunnit aspect, as all suspects are explored until the actual perpetrator is caught. In several episodes, survivors of these harrowing sagas will be revealed to tell their own story, in their words.

programme.title: Fred & Rose West: House Of Horrors
programme.desc: We return to 25 Cromwell Street to reveal never before shared secrets and to shed new light on the relationship between the Wests; uncovering the truth of Rose's part in the crimes and revealing the tragic aftermath of the devastating case.

programme.title: Britain's Darkest Taboos
programme.sub-title: My Mother And Brother Murdered My Father
programme.desc: In 2008, 50-year-old gamekeeper Nigel Bacon was killed by hitman Peter Jacques. Jacques was hired by Nigel's wife, Susan, and son, Michael, in a plot to claim his inheritance.

programme.title: Deadly Kin
programme.desc: Intriguing true crime stories of families gone wrong. It shows that when family bonds break, sometimes frightening forces are unleashed: love can turn to hate, respect to retribution, and protection to harm.

programme.title: Murdered at First Sight
programme.desc: Most killers know their victims, Murdered At First Sight investigates the alarming rise in "Stranger Murders" and the challenges faced by the police in tracking down the suspect of an entirely random homicide.

programme.title: Murdered at First Sight
programme.desc: Most killers know their victims, Murdered At First Sight investigates the alarming rise in "Stranger Murders" and the challenges faced by the police in tracking down the suspect of an entirely random homicide.

programme.title: Cold Blood
programme.desc: Witnesses report a suspicious young man fleeing a Tempe, Arizona townhouse, and moments later a fire erupts inside. Firefighters break down the door and quickly douse the flames. That's when they discover the body of 20-year-old Kathleen Smith. Her face is burned beyond recognition and there's a deep wound on the back of her head. A part-time student with a job at a real estate office, Kathleen was just about to open a restaurant franchise with a childhood friend. Investigators look into both her personal and business affairs and while they do come up with some promising leads, their three main suspects all have alibis and the case goes cold. It will take 15 years and a tip from a notorious murderer before they can finally close in on the cold-blooded killer.

programme.title: Wives With Knives
programme.desc: After Debra Hudson's high school romance fizzles, she marries a restaurant co-worker named Jay. Right from the start, Jay neglects Debra, and she suspects that he's cheating. It turns out Jay's addicted to meth, and he brings her down with him. After her brother's suicide, she goes to rehab, kicks the addiction and kicks Jay out of her life. She goes to school for social work and meets a man named Rick, who seems like the perfect provider. But Rick has a dark side that comes out only after the wedding. He starts physically abusing Debra until she finally musters up the courage to leave. She has an epiphany that her purpose in life is to help others and starts her own non-profit in Texas. Back in her hometown, she meets Jamie Branch, a hot bartender who's 14 years younger. She throws her training to the wind and dives headlong into the relationship, even though it's clear Jamie has both drinking and emotional issues. Soon, Debra falls off the wagon, and her drinking quickly gets out of hand. One night, in a jealous alcohol-fueled rage, she points a gun at Jamie and fires. Fortunately, the safety lock is on. Debra's arrested, but released the following day when Jamie refuses to press charges. Jamie's mum kicks the couple out of the house, and they move into a motel, where Debra gets a job doing landscaping and pruning. One night Debra comes home from work to find Jamie completely plastered. What starts as an argument over Jamie's alcohol use soon spirals into a violent battle. Debra grabs a pair of pruning shears and plunges them into Jamie's leg three times. In spite of the blood, the stab wounds are not life-threatening. Debra serves one year of probation.

programme.title: Fred And Rose West's Secret Murders
programme.desc: Fred and Rose West were convicted of murdering 12 young women. Now, several pieces of strong new evidence have been discovered, indicating there may be many more than these known victims.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: Following police detectives around the clock, each investigation plays out against the time pressure of only being able to hold a suspect for 24 hours.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody

programme.title: Debi Marshall Investigates Frozen Lies
programme.desc: Investigative journalist Debi Marshall explores one of Australia's most sensational murders, the Lawyer in the Freezer. An enquiry leads Debi down a dark path, exposing even more sinister crimes.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A vehicle obstructing traffic is investigated. A young man with a lengthy rap sheet is apprehended. A DUI suspect flees the scene of an accident. Officers try to quell a neighbourly dispute.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: The driver of a stolen vehicle is confronted. A female complainant is taken into custody. Officers respond to an alcohol induced disturbance. Officers visit the home of a quarreling military couple.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: Good Samaritans assist officers with a burglary. Drugs are recovered during a traffic stop. A carjacker is arrested after fumbling his story. An armed man is admonished for having a firearm outside.

programme.title: Cops Reloaded
programme.desc: A woman is questioned after allegedly buying drugs. Drugs are recovered in a traffic stop. A parked vehicle spotted in a drug-infested area is pursued. Some parking lot drug activity is investigated.

programme.title: Wives With Knives
programme.desc: After Debra Hudson's high school romance fizzles, she marries a restaurant co-worker named Jay. Right from the start, Jay neglects Debra, and she suspects that he's cheating. It turns out Jay's addicted to meth, and he brings her down with him. After her brother's suicide, she goes to rehab, kicks the addiction and kicks Jay out of her life. She goes to school for social work and meets a man named Rick, who seems like the perfect provider. But Rick has a dark side that comes out only after the wedding. He starts physically abusing Debra until she finally musters up the courage to leave. She has an epiphany that her purpose in life is to help others and starts her own non-profit in Texas. Back in her hometown, she meets Jamie Branch, a hot bartender who's 14 years younger. She throws her training to the wind and dives headlong into the relationship, even though it's clear Jamie has both drinking and emotional issues. Soon, Debra falls off the wagon, and her drinking quickly gets out of hand. One night, in a jealous alcohol-fueled rage, she points a gun at Jamie and fires. Fortunately, the safety lock is on. Debra's arrested, but released the following day when Jamie refuses to press charges. Jamie's mum kicks the couple out of the house, and they move into a motel, where Debra gets a job doing landscaping and pruning. One night Debra comes home from work to find Jamie completely plastered. What starts as an argument over Jamie's alcohol use soon spirals into a violent battle. Debra grabs a pair of pruning shears and plunges them into Jamie's leg three times. In spite of the blood, the stab wounds are not life-threatening. Debra serves one year of probation.
